skill.name: lipinski-rule-filter
skill.description: Filter compound libraries based on Lipinski's Rule of Five for drug-likeness.
skill.summary: Filter compound libraries based on Lipinski's Rule of Five for drug-likeness.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 55 static analysis findings were evaluated as false positives. Weak cryptographic algorithm flags matched 'Lipinski' (a drug-likeness rule name, not crypto) and 'Descriptors' (RDKit molecular descriptor module, not crypto). Shell execution flags all reference markdown code blocks in SKILL.md showing example bash commands. Path traversal flags are from a security checklist that warns against path traversal. System reconnaissance flags are informational print statements and markdown documentation. Network URL flags are legitimate references to RDKit documentation and the source repository. The script reads SMILES compound data from files and writes filtered results with no network calls, dynamic execution, or credential handling.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm false positive - Lipinski is a drug-likeness rule
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Lipinski' and 'Descriptors' as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are RDKit cheminformatics module names for molecular property calculation, not cryptographic operations. Lipinski's Rule of Five evaluates drug-likeness (MW, LogP, HBD, HBA). No crypto code exists in this skill.

low_findings.title: Shell backtick execution false positive - markdown code blocks
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 28 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. All occurrences are inside markdown fenced code blocks (```bash ... ```) showing example commands for user reference. No active shell execution occurs. The commands include python compilation checks, help flags, and usage examples.

low_findings.title: Path traversal false positive - security checklist warnings
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'path traversal sequence' at SKILL.md lines 128 and 131. These lines are inside a Security Checklist section that warns against path traversal. The text reads 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)' as security recommendations. No actual path traversal code exists.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance false positive - informational print statements
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged informational print and documentation statements as 'System reconnaissance'. The flagged locations include output path printing in main.py (Output files, summary counts) and markdown text in SKILL.md (workflow descriptions, parameter documentation, test case labels). These are transparent progress messages and documentation, not reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL false positive - legitimate documentation references
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged URLs in references/lipinski_references.md and SKILL.md as 'Hardcoded URL' network indicators. These are legitimate references to the RDKit documentation website (rdkit.readthedocs.io, rdkit.org) and the source GitHub repository. No malicious or exfiltration endpoints detected.

content.user_title: Filter Drug-Like Molecules with Lipinski Rule of Five
content.value_statement: Quickly identify drug-like compounds from SMILES data using Lipinski's Rule of Five. This skill automates molecular property calculations for compound libraries to prioritize promising leads in early drug discovery.
actual_capabilities: Evaluate single compounds against Lipinski's Rule of Five from SMILES strings
Filter large compound libraries from CSV, TSV, or SMILES format files
Generate detailed violation reports listing which rules each compound failed
Customize the maximum allowed rule violations threshold from 0 to 4
Calculate molecular weight, LogP, H-bond donors, and H-bond acceptors using RDKit
Export filtered results in CSV or SMILES format with pass or fail annotations
limitations: Requires RDKit library (rdkit-pypi) for molecular property calculations
Does not evaluate other drug-likeness rules such as Veber or Ghose filters
Cannot process 3D molecular structures, conformers, or coordinate files
Limited to SMILES-based molecular representation only

use_cases.title: Pre-Screen Compound Libraries for Synthesis Prioritization
use_cases.description: Filter thousands of compounds from a virtual library before synthesis to focus resources on molecules likely to have good oral bioavailability.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Drug Candidates in Early Development
use_cases.description: Check individual molecules against Lipinski rules during early-stage drug development to prioritize promising leads for further testing.

use_cases.title: Teach Drug-Likeness Principles in Academic Settings
use_cases.description: Demonstrate Lipinski Rule of Five by running calculations on known drugs in a classroom setting and identifying rule violations.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Single Compound for Drug-Likeness
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if aspirin with SMILES CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O passes Lipinski's Rule of Five.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick evaluation of a known drug molecule

prompt_templates.title: Filter a Compound Library from a CSV File
prompt_templates.prompt: Filter the compounds in compounds.csv using Lipinski's Rule of Five and save passing compounds to filtered.csv.
prompt_templates.scenario: Batch processing a compound library file

prompt_templates.title: Use Relaxed Violation Threshold for Broader Screening
prompt_templates.prompt: Filter library.smi and allow up to 2 Lipinski violations. Save the detailed report to results.txt.
prompt_templates.scenario: Using relaxed filtering criteria for early-stage screening

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Simple Molecule as a Learning Exercise
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the SMILES string CCO for ethanol and explain which Lipinski rules it passes or violates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Learning exercise with a simple molecule

output_examples.input: Check if aspirin (SMILES: CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O) passes Lipinski's Rule of Five.
output_examples.output:
Compound: Aspirin - PASSED with 0 violations.
Molecular Weight: 180.16 Da (under 500 limit)
LogP: 1.31 (under 5 limit)
H-bond Donors: 1 (under 5 limit)
H-bond Acceptors: 3 (under 10 limit)
Aspirin is fully drug-like according to all four Lipinski rules.

output_examples.input: Filter the compounds in compounds.csv and show a summary.
output_examples.output:
Processed 50 compounds.
Passed: 42 out of 50 (84%)
Failed: 8 out of 50 (16%)
Common violations: High molecular weight in 5 compounds, high LogP in 3 compounds.
Detailed report and filtered output have been saved.

best_practices: Validate input SMILES strings before batch processing to avoid parsing errors in large libraries.
Review failed compounds individually since some drug candidates intentionally violate one Lipinski rule.
Use CSV format with SMILES and Name columns for clear processing of large compound libraries.
anti_patterns: Do not use Lipinski Rule of Five as the sole filter for drug candidate selection without considering other ADME properties.
Do not apply the filter to non-oral drug candidates such as injectables or topical treatments where the rules do not apply.
Do not process extremely large libraries without first validating the SMILES format for consistency across all entries.

faq.question: What is Lipinski Rule of Five?
faq.answer: It predicts poor drug absorption when a compound violates more than one of four rules: molecular weight over 500, LogP over 5, H-bond donors over 5, and H-bond acceptors over 10.

faq.question: What input formats does this tool support?
faq.answer: It accepts SMILES strings directly or reads from CSV, TSV, and SMILES files (.smi, .smiles) with auto-detection of the format.

faq.question: How are rule violations counted?
faq.answer: Each rule violation counts separately. A compound that exceeds both the MW threshold and LogP threshold has 2 violations.

faq.question: Can I change the default violation limit?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the violations parameter to set a custom maximum from 0 for strict filtering up to 4 with all rules relaxed.

faq.question: Does this tool need any external dependencies?
faq.answer: Yes, it requires RDKit (rdkit-pypi) for molecular property calculations. Install it with pip install rdkit-pypi.

faq.question: What is the difference between passed and failed compounds?
faq.answer: Passed compounds have violations at or below the maximum threshold. Failed compounds exceed that limit and get a detailed violation report.
